The underlying moisture and carbon content values for the in-going and out-going products are available on the Input and Output tabs for review. Under Allocation, you can also navigate to the calculation details and formula of the carbon balance.

 

To support this capability, the Import Business Transactions (Corporate and Products) API has also been enhanced with the new moistureContent and carbonContentPerUnit fields. For more information, see Calculating Carbon Balance and the scenario guide Calculating Scope 1 Emissions (Carbon Content) Using Business Transaction Data on SAP Help.

 

Expanded Gas Impact Categories for Environmental Footprint Analysis

In previous updates, I introduced how SAP Sustainability Footprint Management is evolving from a carbon-focused solution into a broader environmental footprint platform (see: Deep Dive: Multi-Impact Categories in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management). This quarter, we’re continuing that journey with seven new gas-related impact categories for environmental footprint calculations:

  • CO2 – Carbon Dioxide (Total Biogenic and Fossil)
  • CH4 – Methane (Total Biogenic and Fossil)
  • N2O – Nitrous Oxide
  • HFCs – Hydrofluorocarbons (Aggregated GHGs)
  • PFCs – Perfluorocarbons (Aggregated GHGs)
  • SF6 – Sulphur Hexafluoride
  • NF3 – Nitrogen Trifluoride

This gives you more transparency into the constituent gases behind the overall greenhouse gas (GHG) picture, with each gas calculated using its own characterization factors based on global warming potential, and separate biogenic and fossil treatment for CO2 and methane. For more information on the gases, see Impact Categories on SAP Help.

In the Manage Emission Factors app, you can maintain emission factors for the new gas categories either directly in the UI or by importing them via the app template, as with the other impact categories.

In the Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes app, you activate the relevant impact categories for your scope. With this release, you can activate up to 12 impact categories, including Climate Change, which remains enabled by default. Once scoped and calculated, the new categories are visible in the Manage Footprint Results app, where the system shows total footprints and statuses across the selected impact categories and the contribution of GHG categories into them.

Constituent Gases in the Item Footprint

 

Footprint Data Offloading

As customers run footprint calculations period after period, the volume of calculated inventory data grows over time. To help you manage that data lifecycle, this quarter we introduced the new Manage Footprint Data Offloads app, which lets you move calculated footprint inventories to long-term storage once they are no longer needed in the active system. This helps you optimize system performance, reduce license consumption, and support your organizational sustainability objectives, all while keeping the data available for the duration you need it.

To make this controllable, footprint data offloading is built around two configurable time periods that you now set per scope in the Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes app:

  • The residence period is the minimum time that calculated footprint inventory data stays in the system before it becomes eligible for offloading to long-term storage. It must be at least 24 months and is part of the overall retention period.
  • The retention period is the total time the data must be kept, including both the residence period in the system and the time in long-term storage. It must also be at least 24 months. After the retention period ends, you can permanently delete the data.

The Manage Footprint Data Offloads app lists all calculated footprint inventories with the status Calculated Successfully, Calculated with Errors, or Calculated. Once an inventory’s residence period has ended, you can offload it from this app, and you can also delete the data of calculated footprint inventories to manage your data lifecycle and system resources. Offloaded inventories can be downloaded as JSON files, so the data remains accessible for archiving, reporting, or audit purposes after it leaves the active system. Offloading actions are also captured in the audit logs.

Manage Footprint Data Offloads

If you’re already a customer, this transition is handled for you: any footprint inventories created before this feature are automatically migrated, with the residence period set to 24 months and the retention period set to 36 months by default. You can adjust these periods per scope going forward to match your own data governance and reporting requirements. Note that to use the app, you need the relevant footprint data and calculation roles. For more information, see Offloading Calculated Footprint Inventories in the Release Impact Information on SAP Help.

 

Emission Factors: Expanded Default Content & Sustamize Partnership

High-quality emission factors remain the foundation for accurate and audit-ready footprint results. This quarter, we broadened the emission factor content from two complementary angles: we refreshed the default content and we added new premium partner content that you can purchase through the SAP Store. As always, the data flows into the Manage Emission Factors app, so you can mix SAP-provided content, partner content, and your own imported data packages within one consistent governance and calculation model.

Default Content from ADEME and EPA

Also this quarter, we further extended the default emission factor content that SAP Sustainability Footprint Management delivers out of the box. We’ve added emission factors from the French Agency for Ecological Transition (ADEME), which is the official public reference database of emission factors for France, organized along the GHG Protocol’s Scope 1, 2, and 3 structure and used as the basis for regulatory greenhouse gas reporting in the French market. Adding ADEME as default content gives you well-recognized, France-relevant factors out of the box, which is particularly valuable if you operate in or source from France.

Furthermore, you can now use new versions of emission factors from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), spanning across the years 2015, 2018, 2020-2022, and 2025. The EPA packages are U.S.-focused and include content for GHG Scopes 1, 2, and 3.

Taken together, this is a substantial expansion of what you get out of the box: all default content draws on six different open-source databases and comprises around 45,300 individual datasets, across UK, US, France, Germany, as well as Europe and Global geographies. The datasets cover Energy, Fuels, Purchased Products, Passenger Travel, Transport of Goods, Process Emissions, and Refrigerants, giving you much wider coverage across categories and regions before you need to bring in your own or partner content. As before, this default content is kept separate from any factors you import yourself: if you don’t maintain your own datasets you can rely on the default content directly, and if you do, you continue to import, release, upgrade, archive, and delete your own data packages alongside it within the same governance model. For more information, see Using Default Emission Factor Data in SAP Help.

Partner Content from Sustamize

Beyond the content SAP delivers directly, we’re also constantly exploring partnership options and I’m happy to share that Sustamize emission factor datasets are now available through the SAP Store: Sustamize Scope 3 Data / Carbon Footprint Data. Sustamize provides high-quality, fully regionalized CO2e emission factors for product carbon footprints and Scope 3 calculations, with over 10 million emission factors across materials, components, and processes. The datasets are continuously updated, validated, and aligned with standards such as the GHG Protocol and ISO 14067, and they integrate directly into SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for scalable, audit-ready emissions calculations.

This partnership means you can:

  • Access a large, granular CO2e database across materials, processes, and regions.
  • Strengthen auditability and compliance with standardized data.
  • Increase accuracy with regional electricity consumption mix data.
  • Enable automated Scope 3 and product carbon footprint (PCF) calculations at scale.
  • Stay current with regularly updated datasets reflecting real-world conditions.

Because this content is consumed through the same Manage Emission Factors app as the rest of your data packages, you can adopt it without changing how you scope, calculate, or govern your footprints.

 

Other Release Highlights

In the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app, you can now enter a plant when creating or importing purchased product group footprints. The plant represents geographical information, such as country or region, so you can define emission factors for a product group at plant level and better capture geographical differences. This works together with the Purchased product footprint on the product group level determination method in the Manage Calculation Variants app, and if both a plant-specific and a non-plant-specific footprint exist for the same product group, the system uses the more specific footprint with plant assignment. See Creating Footprints for Purchased Products and Product Groups on SAP Help for more details.

The Waste Generated in Operations app now displays a subtotal row at the end of each group in the table, showing combined Waste Quantity and Total Footprint values so you can see each group’s total contribution without manually adding individual rows. For more information, see Analyzing Footprints for Waste Generated in Operations on SAP Help.

The Manage Locations app now includes a read-only field showing which address fields were used to calculate geocoordinates. The system first uses all available address fields and, if that returns no result, retries using only country/region and postal code, so minor address issues no longer necessarily prevent a location from receiving accurate geocoordinates. For more details, see Manage Locations on SAP Help.

On the API side, the existing Item Footprints API has been renamed to Item Footprints - V1 and is now deprecated (bug fixes only), while a new Item Footprints - V2 API is available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub with a similar payload structure but improved performance for bulk operations. The batch size capacity for the Item Footprints API has also been expanded from 1,000 to 10,000 records. See APIs for Analytics on SAP Help for all details. In addition, the Import Business Transactions (Corporate and Products) API now includes the new moistureContent and carbonContentPerUnit fields supporting the carbon balance calculation. For more information, see APIs for Data Import on SAP Help.

 

Outlook: Footprint Optimization Agent & CBAM Solution

Beyond the features that shipped this quarter, I also want to share a brief outlook on where we’re heading, across both AI and regulatory topics. For staying updated on all planned innovations, I recommend checking out SAP Road Map Explorer.

Footprint Optimization Agent

At SAP Sapphire this May, SAP announced a new family of sustainability AI agents, planned for general availability by the end of 2026, that embed carbon, compliance, and safety intelligence directly into everyday business workflows rather than treating sustainability as a separate, manual exercise. The line-up spans regulatory readiness, packaging compliance, hazard classification and labeling, workplace safety, and footprint optimization. Read here the full press release: The Path to the Autonomous Enterprise: SAP Announces New Sustainability AI Agents.

The Footprint Optimization Agent is part of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and is designed to help you move from footprint insights to informed action with real impact. It lets you review your corporate and product carbon footprints faster and in more detail, analyzing emission sources across multiple dimensions – products, processes, plants, and value-chain stages – on a consistent calculation foundation, so you can identify footprint hotspots and the reduction levers that matter most.

From there, you can run what-if scenarios to examine how operational choices such as production methods, energy inputs, and sourcing patterns would affect your footprint, and compare emission-reduction scenarios side by side against your baseline. Because all simulations rely on the same underlying footprint data and shared assumptions, the results are directly comparable, helping you understand whether a change reduces impact and by how much. The agent can then generate executive-ready views of footprint calculations, drivers, scenarios, and comparisons, giving sustainability, operations, and finance teams a shared starting point to prioritize high-impact initiatives, map the financial impact, and track progress against reduction goals. The Footprint Optimization Agent is planned to be generally available in Q4 2026, so I’ll come back to it in more detail once it’s released. For more details, see the business case Reduce Carbon Footprint More Efficiently with AI.

The following video is part of this year's sustainability showcase at SAP Sapphire and gives you a preview of how AI agents automate reporting - creating data points and linking metrics to disclosures with traceable lineage. It shows how you can use these agents to identify carbon hotspots, simulate scenarios, and recommend actions balancing cost and risk (Footprint Optimization Agent starts at 3:22).

 

The Footprint Optimization Agent is however only one part of how we’re bringing AI into the product. We’re also continuing to invest in the AI capabilities already embedded in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. For the AI-assisted emission factor mapping we plan to release significant improvements in July already to make mapping your products, materials, and business transactions to suitable emission factors faster, more accurate, and easier to govern. Stay tuned for the details.

CBAM Solution

With the CBAM definitive regime having started on January 1, 2026, we also announced new Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) capabilities planned for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management together with SAP Green Ledger later this year. CBAM reporting requires total embedded emissions that are consistent, repeatable, and traceable, often across thousands of shipments and suppliers, which is exactly the kind of scaled calculation SAP Sustainability Footprint Management is built for.

The planned CBAM capabilities in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management will include centralizing ERP and master data enhanced for CBAM purposes alongside official CBAM content, automating the interpretation and use of supplier data, calculating embedded emissions, certificates, and estimated CBAM cost, a CBAM dashboard to view and analyze emissions and costs, and the generation of reports ready for download and submission to the EU. The exact cost calculation will be based on the weekly EU ETS price in SAP Green Ledger, which accounts for the financial liability triggered by CBAM-covered emissions and the certificates used to settle it, in line with IFRS and US GAAP. Together, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Green Ledger connect emissions data with the financial dimensions companies already use, so carbon can be governed, allocated, forecasted, audited, and reconciled in line with financial data. The first release is planned for Q4 2026, and I’ll share more details in the next months. To learn more about CBAM and our solution, read the following blog: CBAM Definitive Regime: What to Know and Actions to Take.

 

Analyst Recognition: IDC MarketScape Leader

I’m also happy to share that for the second time SAP has been recognized as a Leader in the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Carbon Accounting and Management Applications 2026 Vendor Assessment. The assessment evaluated 17 vendors on how well they support credible emissions measurement, strong data governance, corporate-, product-, and supplier-level visibility, and standards-based reporting. It singles out SAP’s ERP-embedded approach, which “unifies financial, operational, and sustainability data into a single, trusted foundation”, as a key strength. SAP Sustainability Footprint Management is a core part of this suite, enabling organizations to calculate carbon footprints across Scopes 1, 2, and 3. It’s great external validation of the direction many of the features in these updates are taking. For more information and downloading the report, see the SAP News Feature: SAP Named a Leader in the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Carbon Accounting and Management Applications 2026 Vendor Assessment.

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

To see what’s ahead, the SAP Road Map Explorer gives you a forward-looking view of planned innovations for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. And if you’d like to help shape the product, the SAP Influence portal is the place to submit improvement requests and vote on ideas from other customers and partners – many of the enhancements I cover in these posts started as customer requests there.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-day trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through the features and functions comprehensively.

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities - Third Anniversary Update
entry.content:
Three years ago already, we have launched the SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities as our single channel for collecting your suggestions for improvements related to SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

For background information, please refer to my previous blog post New SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

To mark this milestone, I'm pleased to share a status update on our progress.

Session Statistics (as of 1 July 2026)

In total we have 149 improvement requests (IRs) in our session.

51 IRs delivered – including 23 delivered during the last twelve months, , starting with those that have received most votes:

  • IR 351738 - Add Fields 'Market Location' and 'Metering Location' for App 'Resolve Implausible Meter Readings' 
  • IR 364511 - Request for Better Error Management in IS-U PF Process 
  • IR 357184 - §40 EnWG gesetzliche Verpflichtung zur aktiven Übermittlung einer Verbrauchsübersicht 
  • IR 357107 - Re-plausibilisation for later meter readings (ISU_DM_MR_FUTURE_PLAUSI active) 
  • IR 355596 - RTP-Schnittstelle mit Eingabevariable in Komponente / RTP-Interface with Input variable ... 
  • IR 354708 - Pre-filling of fields during the connection object search by address (Customer Engagement/ IC Agent) 
  • IR 354703 - Additional search options are needed in the free-text search of premise(Customer Engagement/IC Agent 
  • IR 330456 - Enterprise search for utilities objects 
  • IR 316432 - EDM: Customizing of profile version creation is not considered during settlement run 
  • IR 346592 - Fälligkeitsdatum beim Druckbeleg-Storno  
  • IR 373238 - Fiori IS-U Overview App based on a search (e.g Address, Customer, Point of Delivery, Installation) 
  • IR 317134 - EEDM02 transaction in Fiori 
  • IR 328753 - Deleting device info records en masse 
  • IR 345970 - Enhancement of Transaction EEDMPROFVALCHECK 
  • IR 346630 - Transaktion EEDMPROFVALCHECK Option “nur abrechnungsfähige Profilstatus“ 
  • IR 358288 - set due date of budget billing plan during invoicing 
  • IR 348044 - Profile Status and Status of Billing Orders do not correspond  
  • IR 352844 - Felder für Biller Direct sind in Customer Engagement nicht verfügbar 
  • IR 368454 - Allow meter reader (Ableser) in combination with meter reading type „Meter reading by the customer“ 
  • IR 331497 - MDUS Additional Attributes 
  • IR 346808 - Versorgungsanlage im Datenumfeld farblich differenzierbar Request 
  • IR 349323 - Expand utilities sales contract search with POD external ID 
  • IR 357685 - Analyzing Meter Reading Records with Reason Code 09 with Missing Gas Data 

For a complete list of all delivered improvement requests, visit: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3504/ideas-completed

3 IRs accepted and planned to be delivered in Q3–Q4 2026:

  • IR 363172 - Increase the maximum character limit for the “Code for Identifying a Register” 
  • IR 366770 - EG72 Device allocation additional start time and end time/ EG72 Gerätezuordnung zusätzl. Uhrzeit 
  • IR 356178 - Replacement of iMS (+smgw to mME)-Enablement of ami ws for assignment of base meter to MaLo/MeLo 

4 IRs marked as Planned (Long-Term) – identified as larger investments and included in the SAP S/4HANA Utilities product roadmap:

  • IR 330477 - Flexible Aggregation von Lastgängen / Flexible aggregation of load profiles 
  • IR 351296 - Fiori Billing and Metering Reading Overview/Analysis Apps 
  • IR 323237 - Public API for Developer Extensibility: RAP Interface for Utilities Contract Accounts /V... 
  • IR 352899 - SAP Fiori App for Contract Account Creation/Change/Display 

5  IRs under review.

19 IRs with Acknowledged status, including 5 with 7+ votes (approaching the 10-vote threshold for review)

2 IRs requiring additional information.

64 IRs not planned: Most rejected due to insufficient votes within 12 months or being outside the scope of utilities-specific functionality.

Important Reminders

This session focuses exclusively on utilities-industry specific features in SAP S/4HANA. For improvement requests related to other SAP S/4HANA topics, please see How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and the SAP Continuous Influence Sessions overview.

Please note that a valid S user is required to participate in our (and other) SAP Continuous Influence session(s). For details see knowledge base article 3014377 - Accessing the Continuous Influence Session "SAP S/4HANA Utilities". 

Thank You

We appreciate  for your active participation and engagement. Your input helps us to continuously improve SAP S/4HANA Utilities according to your business requirements.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SCM Enablement: Live Product Expert Sessions in 2026
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their journey toward the Autonomous Enterprise, SAP is redefining how business processes are executed through AI, automation, and data-driven decision-making. With SAP’s recent Autonomous Enterprise vision now rolled out, enabling users to fully leverage SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Supply Chain Management (SCM) solutions has become more important than ever.

We at Cloud ERP & SCM Product Success Enablement and Adoption strive to enable our valued customers and partners to take advantage of our suite solutions. Therefore, we offer various enablement assets, from product experts to product experts, explaining the features and functions as well as the benefits of innovations across the suite.

In this blog, we showcase the diverse live session formats delivered for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP Supply Chain Management. From expert-led webinars to deep-dive learning experiences, these programs are designed to help customers and partners stay current with innovations, build practical expertise, and accelerate adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SCM solutions. Explore how each format supports different learning needs and empowers participants to get the most value from their SAP investments.

 

In the following, I'd like to shed some light on our various live product expert sessions. As of today, the team offers five different types of live sessions:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Early Release Series | Equivalent: What’s New for SAP SCM
  • In-Depth with SAP Cloud ERP | Equivalent: Meet the Expert for SAP SCM
  • Product Expert Training for the Autonomous Enterprise

 

This table helps you to get an overview of the available sessions as well as the content that is covered during the different formats. If you're interested in more detail and would like to follow regular updates, check out the individual landing pages linked below.

Live Product Expert Session

Content Covered

Frequency

Target Audience

Access Requirements

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Early Release Series

Talk about and demo key innovations for upcoming major SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition releases

Bi-yearly

Customers, partners, SAP Internals

Active SAP Learning Hub License needed*

In-Depth with SAP Cloud ERP

Deep-dive knowledge transfer sessions independently from releases for both public and private cloud

Monthly based on demand

Customers, partners, SAP Internals

Active SAP Learning Hub License needed*

What’s New for SAP SCM

Talk about and demo key innovations for major SAP SCM releases

Bi-yearly

Customers, partners, SAP Internals

ON24 webinars, open & free for anyone

Meet the Expert for SAP SCM

Deep-dive knowledge transfer sessions independently from releases for SAP SCM

Monthly based on demand

Customers, partners, SAP Internals

ON24 webinars, open & free for anyone

Product Expert Training H1

Snackable standalone expert videos covering broad and deep-dive product knowledge across the suite

Always-on in H1 starting mid-March – end of June

Open to anyone

Via learning.sap.com

Product Expert Training H2

~ 3 full weeks of training covering broad and deep-dive product knowledge across the suite

Hybrid event in H2 (starting 28th Sept. 2026)

Partners and SAP internals

Active SAP Learning Hub License needed**

*Get access to Support Edition for Customers & Partners // Internal Edition for SAP Employees.
**Access Partner Edge here or send a mail to partner@sap.com if you face any issues.

 

Live Product Expert Sessions Throughout 2026

Fig. 1: Global enablement plan for 2026; based on SAP’s release plan. As the release plan may be changed by SAP without notice, the global enablement plans can change as well.

 

With the above focus of the sessions in mind, let's take a look at the global live session plan for 2026. For the two major releases SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, we plan on showing and demoing highlights of the releases during the Early Release Series which is happening one week before shipment.

Similar to the offering for the Public Cloud, we host a webinar series to cover the latest features in the area of SAP SCM via the What’s New format.

In an almost monthly cadence, based on demand, we're hosting In-Depth with SAP Cloud ERP which is a format that is running independently from releases. In these sessions, we cover specific topics in-depth to get a more holistic insight into certain aspects of the product. As this series is not bound to releases, it depends on topic suggestions from our stakeholders. If you'd like to nominate topics, simply do so via this blog post.

Similar to this In-Depth series, we host an event called Meet the Expert which is targeted for deep dive insights into SAP SCM solutions.

In H1 2026, we offer the Product Expert Training in an always-on format with standalone expert videos and bite-sized learning content covering the Autonomous Suite. In the second half of the year (H2) 2026, the Product Expert Training for the Autonomous Suite will take place in a hybrid mode. The event will combine an onsite week in St. Leon-Rot with multiple weeks of virtual sessions, enabling partners to engage directly with SAP product experts and executives in person, while also allowing broader participation through live online access.

 

 

How to sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support Edition as a customer/partner

In order to watch the recordings, you will need to have an active SAP Learning Hub license with at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition. Find out how to register for the SAP Enterprise Support edition in case you don't already have access to any of the editions yet:

  • Navigate to the sign up page
  • Click the Sign up button. Authenticate yourself with your s-user ID and password. Upon first access, the system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalog respectively. Within two hours, you will then receive a registration confirmation via e-mail.
  • After you have received the confirmation e-mail, the registration process is complete.

Partners can access the recordings through an SAP Partner Edge Edition.

 

How to sign up for the Internal Edition as an SAP employee

In order to watch the recordings, you will need to have an SAP Learning Hub license. In case you have no edition assigned to your profile, please register here for the Internal Edition which is free of charge.

Make sure to be logged onto the SAP Learning Hub before opening the URL. In case the link doesn't open, copy and paste it into your browser. If you face any other issues with your login, please contact the help center.

 

Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

 

Find All of Our Assets

Is there more enablement we’re offering? Of course! We offer a podcast, different video formats, demo playlists, customer councils and much more. Check out our interactive wheel:

entry.title: Support Accreditation Unit 2 Lesson: Resolving Issues with Troubleshooting and Diagnostic Tools
entry.content:
This blog provides concise details on various troubleshooting and diagnostics tools to enable you to solve challenges across your SAP landscape. This is an excerpt from the Support Accreditation learning course Unit 2 Lesson 2. The tools we will deep-dive into are:

  • Support Log Assistant
  • Automated Search for SAP Notes
  • Performance Assistant for SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles Search
  • Kernel Snapshot Analyzer
  • SAP BusinessObjects BI Support Tool
  • Flexible Log Reader

Ready to level up your SAP support journey? Get Support Accredited and unlock the full power of self-service tools, expert tips, and multi-channels. Earn an SAP-certified digital badge to showcase your expertise.  Begin your Support Accreditation journey today!

 

Support Log Assistant

Overview

  • The support log assistant tool analyses text files such as logs, traces, and configuration files automatically for known issues and recommendations.
  • On the SAP for Me customer portal, the support log assistant analysis is activated during case creation, when troubleshooting files are attached.
  • It is also freely available as a standalone self-service web application, powered by the SAP Business Technology Platform, scan log/trace files in your web browser.
  • Identifies log issues and provides references to relevant SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles.

Benefits

  • 24/7 automated, expert-level log file analysis.
  • Accelerated issue resolution without the need to open a support case.
  • Save time by reducing manual log analysis and solution searches.
  • Generates a PDF report of log analysis findings and recommendations.
  • Easily share results with peers to extend best practices and lessons learned.

Access

When you create a case and attach files to it in the "Attachments" section, support log assistant analysis occurs automatically. You can also access the support log assistant as a standalone tool by either:

  • Choosing the "Services & Support" dashboard in SAP for Me.
  • Using this link to connect directly to the standalone tool.

 

You can then use the support log assistant effectively by following the steps below:

  1. Add log files.

     

  2. To scan files, choose Next.

     

  3. Review raised alerts and suggested solutions.

     

  4. Provide feedback on the efficacy of the analysis.

     

  5. Download the "Analysis Report" and "Archive Session Files" if you want to save or share the analysis results.

     

Relevant Links

  • Support log assistant standalone, self-service tool
  • Support log assistant video demo
  • SAP Knowledge Base Article: 2838708 - Using the support log assistant to automate support-related file analysis
  • SAP Help Portal: Support log assistant Wiki
  • List of File Types that can be Analyzed

 

Automated Search for SAP Notes

Overview

  • The Automated Notes Search Tool (ANST) simplifies the identification of SAP correction notes for functional issues, customer codes, and customizing tables, particularly for standard processes.
  • To access SAP-relevant notes and identify issues related to customer code and customizing tables, users can initiate the process by starting the ANST transaction.
  • Additional resources are available for further understanding, including FAQs, instructional videos, and the SAP Help Portal.
  • Explore the following blogs for more insights: ANST and What is ANST?

Benefits

  • The tool only identifies SAP Notes that are relevant to the specific issue, streamlining the search process.
  • Unlike traditional note searches that require specific search terms, this tool automatically searches for SAP Notes based on the ABAP objects involved in the process, simplifying the search.
  • The tool is designed for ABAP-based systems, whether they run directly in the back-end system or through a front end, such as SAP Fiori or portals.

Access

  • Start an ANST transaction from the back-end and reproduce the issue. You can find more information in SAP Knowledge Base Article 1818192.
  • Before you proceed, make sure that the following notes is implemented in your system:2286869. Probably, the note has been applied by support package but manual steps have to be followed anyway.

 

 

Performance Assistant for SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles Search

Overview

  • The Performance Assistant simplifies the search for SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles related to ABAP messages. It's based on the relevant message code, including the message class and number.
  • For additional information, you can check the FAQ, watch an informative video, or access in-depth details in the blog.

Benefits

  • Receive real-time updates for documentation.
  • The Performance Assistant instantly identifies new SAP Notes containing the relevant ABAP message code, ensuring you benefit from immediate updates and information.

Access

  • When you encounter an error message, double-click the error message and, to proceed with additional actions, choose the binoculars or search icon.
  • For more detailed guidance on this process, you can find additional information in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2096401.

 

 

Kernel Snapshot Analyzer

Overview

  • Server snapshots can be triggered automatically or manually.
  • The kernel snapshot analyzer runs over 36 analyzers for different scenarios.
  • Read a blog introducing kernel snapshot and kernel snapshot analyzer on SAP Community.
  • SAP Knowledge Base Article 3110588 explains how the kernel snapshot analyzer helps analyze system-wide issues and provides solutions using server snapshots.

Benefits

  • The tool gathers the most important trace files used for troubleshooting.
  • It streamlines the analysis of ABAP system-wide problems, such as system hang-ups, slow performance, resource bottlenecks, and high business load situations.
  • Additionally, it visualizes errors and provides solutions along with configuration recommendations.

Access

  • A Java-based application is available for download from SAP Knowledge Base Article 3110588.
  • SAP Knowledge Base Article 3122914 provides a step-by-step guide to collecting a server snapshot and analyzing it with the kernel snapshot analyzer.

 

 

SAP BusinessObjects BI Support Tool

Overview

  • The SAP BusinessObjects BI support tool is a simple and easy-to-deploy client for the SAP BusinessObjects BI platform.
  • Its landscape analysis report offers a comprehensive view of the configuration and topology of SAP Business Objects Business Intelligence.
  • The tool automatically checks for SAP Best Practices, system configuration, service distribution, and other alerts.
  • It includes a toolbox with various root cause analysis and administrative tools, making it a lightweight supportability solution that simplifies the work of BI administrators.

Benefits

  • It eliminates question ping-pong and speeds up issue resolution.
  • Historical system configuration capture and change analysis are supported.
  • Admins save time with built-in tools, such as security analyser, authentication, and trace wizards.
  • Proactive issue identification is enabled through customizable landscape alerts.
  • Sensitive report data can be secured for enhanced security.

Access

  • The tool is free for all SAP BusinessObjects customers and can be downloaded via the Software Download Center.
  • Additional information and resources can be found on the SAP Help Portal.
  • There is a version 2.1 webinar and demo available for further insights and demonstrations.

 

 

Flexible Log Reader

Overview

  • A versatile log reading application for analyzing text log files, regardless of their source application
  • Designed for SAP BI platform trace logs (GLFs) and other text formats
  • Features powerful search and filtering options
  • Includes configuration files and expert-created plugins that facilitate automatic analysis and tagging for quick access to critical information
  • A versatile log reading application for analyzing text log files, regardless of their source application

Benefits

  • Uses pre-built categories for quick identification of common SAP BusinessObjects BI log messages.
  • Can efficiently read long chains of multiple logs with minimal memory usage.
  • Capable of opening and scanning large logs, often exceeding the available memory on the machine. For example, a 4GB machine can load approximately 18GB of logs.

Access

  • No installation is required. Download and extract the ZIP file.
  • Run the tool by double-clicking 'FlexiLogReader64.exe'.
  • You can download it from Flexible Log Reader on the SAP Help Portal.

 

Relevant Links

  • SAP KBA 1818192: FAQ for Automated Note Search Tool (ANST)
  • SAP KBA 2096401: FAQ for PANKS
  • SAP KBA 3110588: Kernel snapshot analyzer
  • SAP KBA 2138275: How to generate a landscape analysis report
  • SAP KBA 2203047: How to read and analyze log files

 

Thanks to all the experts from Global Customer Support for putting this content together!

entry.title: Choosing Between SAP Extended Warehouse Management and SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
SAP's warehouse management portfolio now offers three distinct paths: SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud, SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Public Cloud, and the newer warehouse management solution released officially for customers in March 2026: SAP Logistics Management

In this blog, I would like to help customers select the best path between SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud and SAP Logistics Management, based on their intralogistics profile.

What Is SAP Logistics Management?

Before comparing the solutions, let me address the most frequent question I hear from customers: "What is SAP Logistics Management?"

In a nutshell, SAP Logistics Management is a cloud-based logistics solution that combines warehouse management, transportation management capabilities, and Business Network for Logistics to optimize operations through network-centric collaboration. As a new SaaS offering, it unites warehousing and transportation dispatching, helping businesses optimize their logistics fulfillment operations and improve overall efficiency.

Setting the Foundation

This high-level definition serves as an initial foundation for any customer planning to implement an SAP warehouse management solution. The natural next question within the organization is: Which solution best fits my operations?

Both solutions serve distinct warehouse business models, and this statement should guide you on solution evaluation and due to this I would like to provide clarity and help customers avoid common mistakes when deciding between SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud (Embedded or Decentralized) and SAP Logistics Management.

Core Principle

Before starting any discussion, it is essential to understand one key point:

SAP Logistics Management does not replace SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud.

Instead, SAP Logistics Management extends SAP's warehouse portfolio, enabling organizations to optimize every site—not just large and complex distribution centers.

These two solutions serve different intralogistics profiles, with a deliberate overlap zone where either option may be appropriate depending on specific business needs.

The diagram below shows, at a high level, which solution is recommended based on the intralogistics profile. In the overlap zone (highlighted in yellow), both solutions might be applicable. Let us take a deeper look at the different features and scenarios that can guide you toward the right decision

IMPORTANT: Unlike SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud—where new features or enhancements are released with each Feature Package Stack (FPS) or new SAP S/4HANA version—SAP Logistics Management delivers new features and enhancements on a bi-weekly basis. Visit  What’s New Viewer regularly to stay up to date with the latest content.

When to Choose SAP Logistics Management

SAP Logistics Management is best suited for organizations with low-to-mid complexity warehouses. It is designed for smaller warehousing and shipping facilities, scaling businesses, and second-tier operations of large enterprises. It seamlessly integrates with your core SAP S/4HANA Cloud (ERP) order processing and your network of logistics service providers and carriers.

Typical Characteristics

  • Full transparency into logistics operations
  • Scalable growth
  • Ease of access to carrier networks
  • Low-to-medium operational complexity
  • Need for rapid deployment across multiple sites
  • Focus on guided execution rather than deep optimization
  • Desire to eliminate paper-based processes and digitize operations
  • Limited or no advanced automation or robotics
  • Strong emphasis on speed of implementation and lower TCO

Common Warehouse Types

  • Local or regional replenishment centers
  • E-commerce micro-fulfillment centers
  • Production-support warehouses
  • Service and MRO warehouses for internal consumption

When to Choose SAP Extended Warehouse Management

SAP Extended Warehouse Management is designed for highly complex, high-volume warehouse operations requiring advanced optimization and automation.

Typical Characteristics

  • High throughput and performance requirements
  • Peak season processing with large volume spikes
  • Very high task volumes (thousands per hour)
  • Advanced wave management with multiple warehouse requests
  • Intensive use of transport units
  • Integration with warehouse automation technologies

Supported Automation

  • Conveyor systems
  • AS/RS (Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems)
  • AGVs (Automated Guided Vehicles)
  • AMRs (Autonomous Mobile Robots)
  • Material Flow System (MFS)
  • Voice-directed picking

Advanced Capabilities

  • Complex process models (POSC/LOSC)
  • Cross-docking and merchandise flow-through
  • Yard management integration
  • Slotting and rearrangement
  • Task interleaving
  • Labor management and demand planning

The Overlap Zone: When Both Could Fit

Some warehouses fall into a gray area where either SAP Logistics Management or SAP Extended Warehouse Management could be appropriate. In these cases, a deeper evaluation is required.

Choose SAP Logistics Management if:

  • You want a unified platform combining Transportation, Warehouse Management, and Business Network
  • You prefer a modular, low-complexity solution
  • You need a simple execution layer
  • Automation is limited or moderate
  • You prioritize cloud-native architecture
  • You aim for reduced total cost of ownership (TCO)
  • You require a global template with fast rollout capabilities

Choose SAP Extended Warehouse Management if:

  • Your strategy is evolving toward advanced automation
  • You operate in a complex IT landscape integrating multiple ERP systems
  • Advanced routing and picking optimization are must-have capabilities
  • Your warehouse is expected to scale into a high-throughput environment
  • You handle complex exception scenarios
  • You plan to leverage Labor Management, advanced interleaving, or AI-driven optimization

Conclusion

The purpose is not to frame the decision as "old vs. new," but rather as "best fit for each site."

Organizations with diverse warehouse networks may benefit from using both solutions:

  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud for high-throughput and complex distribution centers
  • SAP Logistics Management for smaller, less complex sites that still require structured, digital execution

Ultimately, the key question is not: Which solution is better? But rather: Where is each warehouse today—and where is it going?

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back to this community with an SAP strategic topic: Business AI.

 

            More specifically, Autonomous Finance. AI is the cornerstone of SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering, that revolutionize the way we support our customers businesses: Innovations that develop state-of the-art tools and agents to automate tasks, where humans can be released of cumbersome tasks to focus on business intelligence and financial engineering to maximize benefits with real-time value.

           

            As part of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise, SAP Autonomous Finance is built out of Joule Assistants that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on. The major goal of Financial Closing Assistant is to reducing closing time and streamlines the reporting associated to closing.

 

 

Figure 1: SAP Finance Assistants-Financial Closing Assistant

 

            For years, SAP has developed solutions that enable customers to perform specific business tasks which, while executed outside the ERP system and often requiring significant time and effort, provide critical input for gaining a complete view of the business. Tasks that, if not performed, can disrupt business operations and compromise data visibility. Uploading journal entries is one of them. It is either a periodic or frequent activity or both, in every landscape: satellite systems, legacies, integrations that support the operation in a way or another, that feedback the ERP with transactions that must be posted to keep the accounting books updated.

 

            Many of us have used the different ways to upload journal entries to the ERP: The traditional file (we used to call it "flat") with a fixed structure of columns with specific format. Another is the journal entry template, for postings based on your needs, where fields and values can be selected to produce a tailored-specific template for postings, taking out non-relevant fields and focusing only on the desired ones. This template can also be shared and with pre-defined level of use (group, company, user). Please look at Fiori app Manage Journal Entry Templates (F3803)documentation to know of this, in case you are interested. Both have been extremely useful tools, widely used by customers and partners.

            Month-end journal entries upload is more needed than ever. Increasingly complex system landscapes, new integration points, constantly evolving business models, and the ever-changing business environment demonstrate that, if this process can be executed autonomously with minimal supervision, the resulting efficiency gains and cost savings can make a significant contribution to the organization's value creation efforts. 

What is AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

 

            As period-end journal entries are necessary for period closing, accounting teams are spending considerable time in curating data as per the policies and reporting needs. Through new SAP Fiori apps with Generative AI capabilities embedded, AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition accelerates the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a Financial AI feature to speed-up the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            With AI-assisted Journal Upload, accountants can:

  • Assign a guidance document (written in natural language) for posting to the journal upload case
  • Create journal upload cases
  • Generate journal posting proposals according to the assigned guidance
  • Validate the posting proposals before posting and
  • Post the posting proposal as journals

 

What are typical business cases for AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

            Business scenarios best suited for AI-assisted Journal Upload include recurring general ledger journal entries with a high volume of records and similar information requirements, making them ideal candidates for this autonomous feature. Other excellent use cases include large volumes of prepaid expenses, lease or rental in advance-payments, and customer prepayments, all of which can be efficiently processed using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Is AI-assisted Journal Upload intended for large companies only?

 

            Not at all. Regardless of their size, Companies often pay for services they will receive in the future. Small and large businesses work with prepaid expenses frequently. It is a way to secure better terms, benefit from early-payment discounts, and take advantage of periods of stronger cash flow while ensuring futre obligations are covered.

 

            When the volume of prepaid operations is high, as they happen in advance, a proper tracking is crucial for financial control and compliance, to record them when is right to do it (in the posting period when each service is received). Plus, a posting template easy to use and populate to post to the right accounting objects, is invaluable for saving time and reducing the risk of errors during the financial closing. The easiest, the better. These are some of the advantages of using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

 

            In the graphic below, we find some interesting figures about uploading journal entries for a business (complexity varies in each case). It can be found on SAP Discovery Center - S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - AI-Assisted Journal Upload:

 

 

Figure 2: AI-assisted Journal Upload - Estimated benefits

            Technically speaking, AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an embedded Intelligent Scenario delivered via SAP's Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework, using scenario FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD. It is natively embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, with the underlying AI service provisioned through SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as a backend infrastructure integration — not a side-by-side deployment.

What are the specifications of AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

  • Identifier: J247
  • AI Type: AI Feature
  • Support Component: FI-GL-AI-IJU
  • Minimun Required System Version: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03
  • Required Roles & Authorizations:
    • BTP: Global Account Administrator role, assigned via Security > Role Collections in the BTP Cockpit
    • S/4HANA: ISLM Administrator role to manage intelligent scenario activation
  • BTP Subaccount & Entitlement Requirements:
    • A valid BTP subaccount must exist and be accessible
    • The AI service entitlement must be available and assigned to the relevant subaccount prior to service instance creation

 

What do we need to start using AI-assisted Journal Upload?

 

 

            3 easy steps to determine AI-assisted Journal Upload readiness:

  • Check 1 - To verify the Scenario Status in Intelligent Scenario Management (F4470)  Fiori app, you need to confirm that FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD shows a Published status with no errors in the ISLM app
 
 

 

 

Figure 3: Intelligent Scenario Management

 
 

 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies
Check 2 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Check role authorizations and catalogs if it is not visible

 

 
 

 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Upload Cases
Check 3 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Same considerations as F8539 here.

 

            Also, it is always good to make sure that content in SAP Note 3573144 AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private has been implemented in the system.

            Let me share the link to my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload - How to use it 

            I hope you find this insightful. In my next blog, I will go step by step on how to use AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload - How to use it
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back with the second part of my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload . This time, how to use it.

 

            Remember that AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an AI feature, part of the SAP Autonomous Finance offering.

 

            We also know that SAP Autonomous Finance is one of the Joule Assistants in SAP Autonomous Enterprise platform, and it is there to that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on.

 

           

Start using AI-assisted Journal Upload

 

            AI-assisted Journal Upload requires a couple of IMG-customizing-related activities in the backend, related to the category and subcategory of the journal entries you will post. Following the path Financial Accounting => Financial Accounting Global Settings => Document => Intelligent Journal Uploads, you will see the two activities, Define Journal Upload Case Categories and Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories. Together, they both serve to give a classification to the expenses that better explain your postings.

Define Journal Upload Case Categories:

Figure 1: Define Journal Upload Case Categories

 Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories:

Figure 2: Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories


            Next, to download and adapt the policy template, we access the Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539). With this app, we set up all the relevant fields and the natural language instructions to populate the fields on the template.

 
 

 

Figure 3: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Initial Screen


            Click on Download Template, select the format (Spreadsheet, PDF) and OK. (I feel more comfortable with the excel file, so I chose the first one).

 
 

 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template Downloaded

            Follow the guidelines provided and update the Input instructions that the feature  is going to use to handle the data in the input file. It is important to bear in mind:

  • For now, Cardinality - Optional/Mandatory (O/M)- field is not required. It will be supported in future releases, so you may leave it blank.
  • The file name format must be "Name", followed by a "_", followed by the "Language Code".ext (xslx, pdf). Supported language codes are EN (English), DE (German), ES (Spanish), FR (French), PT (Portuguese), JA (Japanese), ZH (Simplified Chinese). Example: "Policy Document 2026_EN.xlsx".
  • Make sure that the fields referenced in the Input Instruction of a field in the policy document, are columns in the input data file, for example:
 
 

 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template - Input Instruction examples

            Based on the instruction for field WRBTR, there should be a column labeled "Amount" with values in the input file.


            And now, let's work with the policy in the system:

Figure 6: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Creation

Case category and Subcategory: According to customizing mentioned above

Description: Short Text about the policy purpose (max 50 characters)

Valid to: Defaulted to 12/31/9999

Valid from: Starting validity date of the policy. If there is an existing policy of the same Category and Subcategory, with Valid from date prior or equal to this, the system will raise an error, and the policy cannot be saved. The policy must start after the starting date of any previously created policy of this service.

 
 

 

Figure 7: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Activation

 

 

            Once created, the policy needs to be Activated.

            Now, it is the time to upload the input data to post the journal entries.

            We designed a simplified excel file that contains the data of the charges received for shuttle services for 3 employees, to create the postings for the expenses charged for this service:

Figure 8: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Sample input data


            To do that, open the Fiori app Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) and hit Create:

Figure 9: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Updating the Posting Proposal

            Once created, a Proposal must be generated:

Figure 10: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Generating the Posting Proposal

            Proposal Validation:

Figure 11: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Validating the Posting Proposal

            And proposal finally to be posted:

Figure 12: Manage Upload Cases: Posting the Proposal

           

            Let's display the postings in the Fiori app Manage Journal Entries (F0717A):

 

Figure 13: Manage Journal Entries: Displaying the Postings

 

            The posted document SA 1000000006 shows that rides of the three employees using the shuttle bus service in the second week of June 2026 were posted to the right posting period (06/2026), crediting the prepaid expense (which is currently a right, in consequence) for $264,00 USD.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a key component of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering and provides an easy, straightforward process to generate postings in S/4HANA from external sources, as part of the month-end closing, releasing the business experts from spending lots of time and effort fulfilling templates that can be well known, but still somewhat error-prone.

            Before closing, let me share two more useful resources with you.

SAP AI Services in Discovery Center

AI-assisted Journal Upload in help.sap.com

            I hope you find this insightful.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: SAP Green Ledger: Q2-26 Release Updates and Highlights
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q2 2026 edition of our SAP Green Ledger Quarterly Release Highlights! This blog summarizes the most important SAP Green Ledger innovations and enhancements delivered during Q2 2026. Highlights include new capabilities for carbon traceability, carbon collection processing, Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), carbon allocations, analytics, and usability improvements across SAP Green Ledger.

Read on to learn more about the key innovations introduced throughout Q2 2026:

  • Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents
  • Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections
  • Simplified CBAM Processing
  • Flexible Carbon Allocations
  • Analytical Dashboard Update
  • Further Updates and Enhancements

 

Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents

The Manage Carbon Collections app now includes a new "Related Documents" section that allows you to maintain and view references to the SAP S/4HANA documents from which emissions originate. Supported reference types include:

  • Purchasing Documents
  • Accounting Documents
  • Other Documents

If you import material movements into SAP Green Ledger from a connected SAP S/4HANA system, related document references are transferred automatically. When importing carbon data via CSV files, you can also include related document information in the file. For manually created carbon collections, you can maintain related documents directly in the Manage Carbon Collections app. These references are then carried through from the carbon collection into the corresponding journal entries in the Manage Journal Entries app. This allows you to trace carbon postings throughout the accounting process, from the originating business documents to the resulting carbon journal entries, also improving auditability of carbon data.

Related Documents in a Carbon CollectionTo further simplify navigation and reporting, you can now also filter in the Manage Carbon Collections, Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps by Related Document Type and Related Document ID. This helps you quickly locate carbon records for specific business transactions. For more details check SAP Help: Create a Carbon Collection and Use a CSV Template.

 

Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now supports reversing multiple posted carbon collections in a single step. Instead of reversing each carbon collection individually, you can now select and reverse multiple posted carbon collections at once. This significantly reduces manual effort, particularly after correcting mass uploads. Up to 5,000 carbon collections can be reversed in a single operation. For more information, see SAP Help: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal.

 

Simplified CBAM Processing

Several enhancements improve the processing and monitoring of Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) carbon collections while also increasing transparency for CBAM accounts configuration.

Efficient Processing of CBAM Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now also supports reversing CBAM carbon collections. In addition, you can now post and reverse multiple CBAM carbon collections in a single step. As a result, you can process large volumes of carbon collections that require carbon certificates more efficiently.

When reversing CBAM carbon collections, the resulting journal entries are created with the journal entry source type Carbon Collection Reversal, providing a clear audit trail for correction postings. When posting or reversing multiple CBAM carbon collections, SAP Green Ledger automatically calculates a single fair value per company code. This reduces manual effort and ensures consistent valuation across company codes.

To support the monitoring of these postings, the Manage Carbon Liabilities app now supports filtering carbon liabilities by Posted and Reversed status. With that, you can quickly monitor the processing status of carbon liabilities and identify relevant entries. Check out SAP Help for more details: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal, Fair Value Calculation, and View Carbon Liabilities.

Filter for Posted and Reversed Carbon Liabilities in the Manage Carbon Liabilities App

Improved Visibility into EU CBAM Accounts by Company Code

The Configure Carbon Certificate Data app now displays the EU CBAM accounts maintained in the Determine Mandatory Accounts app for each company code. This allows you to view the configured account assignments directly while working with carbon certificate data, without switching between applications. See also SAP Help: Configure Carbon Certificate Data and Determine Mandatory Accounts.

 EU CBAM Accounts per Company Code in Configure Carbon Certificate Data App

 

Flexible Carbon Allocations

Several enhancements make carbon allocations more flexible and align them more closely with financial accounting. Our new capabilities can help you reuse existing allocation logic and support project-based allocation scenarios.

Copy Financial Flow for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The ability to create allocation models by copying financial flows in the Manage Allocations app is now also available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Previously available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this enhancement enables you to create carbon allocation models based on the allocation patterns already used in your connected SAP S/4HANA system.

Allocation Model Copied from Financial Flow

Using the latest Cost Center Accounting data from the connected SAP S/4HANA system, SAP Green Ledger automatically copies the receiving objects and allocation percentages and transfers them into the allocation model. This eliminates the need to manually maintain allocation rules and helps keep carbon allocations aligned with financial allocations. For more details, check SAP Help: Create Allocation Models by Copying from Financial Flow.

Project-Level Carbon Allocations with WBS Elements

The Manage Allocations app now supports Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements as both sender and receiver dimensions when creating periodic allocation models. For both the sender and receiver, you can now choose a WBS element or a cost center as the allocation dimension, enabling allocation scenarios such as WBS-to-WBS, WBS-to-Cost Center, Cost Center-to-WBS, and Cost Center-to-Cost Center. To ensure consistency with the existing allocation behavior for cost centers, the corresponding Profit Center and Segment are now automatically derived as well when a WBS element is used as the receiver dimension.

WBS Elements Used as Sender and Receiver Dimensions in a Periodic Allocation Model

 To further improve transparency, Partner WBS Elements are now included in journal entry line items and displayed in both the Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps. You can also filter, group, and sort by Partner WBS Element in the Display Line Items app, giving you better visibility into carbon allocation flows across project structures. For more details, see Create Period Allocation Models.

 

Analytical Dashboard Update

The SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard has also been updated for Q2 2026 with the latest analytical capabilities for combined financial and carbon reporting. For a detailed overview of these enhancements, see the dedicated SAP Community blog post and the SAP Help Portal topic Analyze Carbon Data Alongside Financial Data.

 

Further Updates and Enhancements

Several smaller enhancements further improve the usability of SAP Green Ledger.

Improved Journal Entry Usability

The Manage Journal Entries app now allows you to maximize the Line Items table. You can review journal entries with many line items more comfortably and reduce the need for scrolling. Additionally, each line item is now assigned a stable unique identifier that remains unchanged when the table is sorted or filtered. The stable identifier makes it easier to reference individual line items and improves navigation during journal analysis.

Updated Posting Terminology

The posting action in the Manage Carbon Collections app has been renamed from Post Carbon Collection to simply Post. The related posting statuses are:

  • Posting
  • Posted
  • Posting Failed
New Origin Business System Filter

A new Origin Business System filter is now available in the Manage Journal Entries, Display Line Items, and Display Account Balances apps. Selecting an origin business system automatically limits the available master data to values from that system. Likewise, when a master data object that is linked to a specific business system, such as a company code, is selected, the corresponding business system is identified automatically and applied to the remaining filters.

 

Additional Information

If you want to dive deeper into SAP Green Ledger, the SAP Help Portal is a good place to start. It provides detailed information on concepts, configuration, and application usage. You can also check the What’s New section, which is updated with every release and gives a complete overview of the latest additions. To see what's coming next, the SAP Road Map Explorer offers insight into planned features and upcoming innovations. It is updated regularly and outlines how the solution will continue to evolve.
